description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage, swept-back low-mounted wings, and a conventional tail assembly. The aircraft features two prominent turbofan engines mounted under each wing, and the wings are equipped with slight upward-angled winglets at the tips. The livery is primarily silver-gray with blue and red accents, and the airline branding is visible on the fuselage and tail fin. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew. The landing gear is visible beneath the fuselage and wings, indicating a tricycle retractable configuration. The overall design is typical of modern long-haul commercial jets, optimized for aerodynamic efficiency and passenger capacity.
description: A large, silver-gray commercial jet airliner with swept low-mounted wings, twin underwing turbofan engines, and a conventional tail. Blue and red accents highlight the fuselage and tail, with visible airline branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, the low-mounted swept wings with underwing engines, and the vertical tail fin with blue and red livery.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetrical placement of the engines under the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the fuselage, wing, and engine arrangement, with clear visibility of the landing gear and tail design.
description_view_3: Top-down view revealing the full wingspan, the placement of the engines, and the overall aerodynamic shape of the airplane.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and upward-angled winglets at the tips.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a nose gear
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows
